ops.title: cc-notes CLI renames flags across versions; both cc-steer and the lab shell it with exact flags, and journal.py's silent degrade hides the breakage — recheck after every cc-notes upgrade
ops.body:
cc-notes v0.24.0 (installed 2026-07-11) renamed `--tag` → `--label` on `log list`/`log add` and dropped `log append -m` in favor of `--entry`/positional TEXT. Two codebases shell out to it with exact flags: `cc-steer-lab/harness/notes.py` (fail-loud `JournalError`) and `cc-steer/cc_steer/journal.py` (degrades every failure to `False` by design, so the nightly pipeline journaling broke **silently**). The tags→labels store migration preserved values — existing logs stay findable by `--label`.

**Why:** This drift stranded the E8 close-out twice: the e8-gen teammate (2026-07-11) most plausibly died on the first `record()` JournalError without reporting, and the first resume attempt burned a ~35-min 8B drafting pass then crashed at the journaling step (drafts were cached, so the retry was cheap). Fixed 2026-07-11 in both repos.

**How to apply:** After any cc-notes upgrade (SessionStart hook prints the version), smoke the shelled interface before long runs: `cc-notes log list --json --label experiment` from ~/Code/cc-steer. If a lab driver dies with `JournalError: ... unknown flag`, it's this class of drift — fix the flag names in `harness/notes.py` / `cc_steer/journal.py`, not the harness contract. Related: [[cc-pushback-ecosystem-map]], [[fix-tooling-breakage-dont-punt]].
